steps.title: Identify the clause structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: The left side—”In 2017, the couple converted a vacant lot in the city into an apiary”—is an independent clause (subject 'couple,' verb 'converted'). The right side—”in the years that followed they acquired nine additional lots and established more than 35 hives”—is also an independent clause (subject 'they,' compound verbs 'acquired' and 'established'), even though it begins with the adverbial phrase 'in the years that followed.'

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: Joining two independent clauses requires a comma + coordinating conjunction, which choice B correctly provides with “apiary, and”. The remaining choices violate this rule: A omits the conjunction, C omits the comma, and D omits both. That matches choice B — B is correct.
